mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 250,000 signals robust economic expansion that boosts corporate earnings expectations, triggering immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of instances when PAYEMS exceeds the 250,000 threshold, or the signal loses predictive power during inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving sustained equity market appreciation over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST diverge, or when the interaction term yields a negative coefficient in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals weakening manufacturing output that reduces corporate profit margins and forces negative S&P 500 adjustments.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions fail to predict negative 20-day returns during late-cycle expansions, or the signal produces false negatives in over 60% of tested cases.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation rising above 2.5% triggers expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive or flat following CPIAUCSL breaches of 2.5%, or the threshold fails to generate statistically significant negative alpha in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with stagnant payroll growth indicates labor market deterioration that suppresses consumer spending forecasts and drives negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined UNRATE and PAYEMS signal does not predict negative 60-day returns, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate revenue projections and equity earnings, producing a consistent positive relationship with S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in industrial production and rise in unemployment signals a supply-demand mismatch that forces rapid equity repricing downward over the following week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly change < -0.5 and UNRATE monthly change > 0.2
failure_condition: The dual-threshold condition fails to predict negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of historical occurrences, or the signal generates excessive false positives during stable growth periods.
